perturbation.original_text: Further the Bidder warrants that its employees, partners, subcontractors, agents, and clients (collectively hereinafter the “Bidder ”) will be similarly bound by this agreement, or by other similar constraints of confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: Further the Applicant warrants that its employees, partners, subcontractors, agents, and clients (collectively hereinafter the “Bidder ”) will be similarly bound by this agreement, or by other similar constraints of confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Bidder” to “Applicant” within the sentence where it refers to those bound by the agreement introduces uncertainty. Although 'Bidder' is still defined at the end of the phrase, 'Applicant' causes confusion about who is warranting and being bound to the agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction that impacts enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure The Bidder shall not publish, or make and/or retain copies or disclose to any third party, any Confidential Information furnished to the Bidder except to such extent as may be necessary to carry out the Bidder ’s duties under this agreement and except to the extent that disclosure is required by judicial process in a legal proceeding, in which event Bidder will provide to the County notice of the request or demand to disclose Confidential Information. Within ten (10) days after award and/or completion of the IFB, or upon demand by the County , all Confidential Information, including all photographs, sketches, models, and drawings related to the desi gn, layout and operation of said project shall be destroyed and files deleted from all of the Bidder’s web sites and electronic devices (including but not limited to computers, cellular phones, etc.). Bidder shall also verify that all Confidential Informa tion provided to partners, subcontractors, agents and clients has
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Bidder" with "Applicant" in the sentence defining who is bound by the agreement, while still using "Bidder" in the original definition at the end of the same phrase. The contradicted text refers specifically to the 'Bidder' and their duties. The change introduces confusion because 'Applicant' is warranting the actions, but the obligations in the contradicted text are placed solely on the 'Bidder', making it unclear who is responsible for confidentiality. This misalignment creates a strong in-text contradiction by creating ambiguity as to who the confidentiality obligations apply to.

Within ten (10) days after award and/or completion of the IFB, or upon demand by the County , all Confidential Information, including all photographs, sketches, models, and drawings related to the desi gn, layout and operation of said project shall be destroyed and files deleted from all of the Bidder’s web sites and electronic devices (including but not limited to computers, cellular phones, etc.). perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Bidder' with 'Respondent' at the beginning of the Disclosure section. However, the rest of the Disclosure section and the contradicted text still refers to 'Bidder'. This creates a clear contradiction regarding which party, the Respondent or the Bidder, is obligated to destroy and delete confidential information. This misalignment of terminology creates ambiguity and directly conflicts with the original intent, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined in the perturbation explanation. This follows the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Bidder agrees to indemnify and h old harmless the County, its elected officials, employees and agents from and against any and all liability, damages, claims, suits, liens, and judgments (including reasonable attorney’s fees), of whatever nature, for injuries to or death of any person or persons, or loss of or damage to property, to the extent attributable to the negligent acts of Bidder , its subcontractors or its respective agents, servants, or employees or such parties’ related to said Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Contractor agrees to indemnify and h old harmless the County, its elected officials, employees and agents from and against any and all liability, damages, claims, suits, liens, and judgments (including reasonable attorney’s fees), of whatever nature, for injuries to or death of any person or persons, or loss of or damage to property, to the extent attributable to the negligent acts of Bidder , its subcontractors or its respective agents, servants, or employees or such parties’ related to said Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Bidder' with 'Contractor' at the start of Section 3 while the rest of the section still referes to 'Bidder' introduces ambiguity on who is responsible for the indemnification. In this perturbation, there is a term shift that introduces a contradiction in which introduces different obligation on different parts. This could lead to challenges in determining liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Indemnification Contractor agrees to indemnify and h old harmless the County, its elected officials, employees and agents from and against any and all liability, damages, claims, suits, liens, and judgments (including reasonable attorney’s fees), of whatever nature, for injuries to or death of any person or persons, or loss of or damage to property, to the extent attributable to the negligent acts of Bidder , its subcontractors or its respective agents, servants, or employees or such parties’ related to said Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Bidder' with 'Contractor' at the start of the section, while the rest of the section, including the contradicted text, still refers to 'Bidder'. This creates a misalignment of terminology, leading to ambiguity about who is responsible for indemnification, as different parts of the clause refer to different entities, thus introducing a contradiction based on misaligned terminology, which adheres to the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement validity, construction and effect shall be governed under the substantive law of the Commonwealth of Virginia without the giving effect to any choice of law provision. Bidder expressly waives any objection to venue or jurisdiction of the L oudoun County Circuit Court, Loudoun County, Virginia.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement validity, construction and effect shall be governed under the substantive law of the Commonwealth of Virginia without the giving effect to any choice of law provision. Provider expressly waives any objection to venue or jurisdiction of the L oudoun County Circuit Court, Loudoun County, Virginia.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Bidder' with 'Provider' in Section 5 introduces ambiguity about which entity is waiving objection to venue and jurisdiction. This inconsistent usage of terminology undermines the clarity of the governing law and jurisdiction clause, potentially impacting the enforceability of the agreement. The original is referring to bidder, while this perturbation introduces a different meaning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law This agreement validity, construction and effect shall be governed under the substantive law of the Commonwealth of Virginia without the giving effect to any choice of law provision. Provider expressly waives any objection to venue or jurisdiction of the L oudoun County Circuit Court, Loudoun County, Virginia.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Bidder' to 'Provider' introduces a misaligned terminology issue. The original text clearly defines which entity waives objection to venue and jurisdiction. However, the change introduces 'Provider' in the same context, creating confusion about which entity is actually waiving these rights. The contradicted text already uses 'Provider', therefore this text now has two different meanings for 'Provider', which causes a contradiction. This contradiction falls under the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, leading to conflicting obligations as it's unclear whose jurisdiction is being waived.
